message:
fix(lua): table.sort/insert/remove now mutate caller's variable

Changed call_builtin/call_function to take &mut [LuaValue] so table
mutation functions modify args[0] in-place. The Expr::Call handler
writes the mutated value back to the caller's env when the first
argument is a named variable. Removed the no-op write_back_table_arg.

Added 11 conformance tests for table mutation (2687 total cases).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -15,13 +15,13 @@ Non-negotiable policy:

exclusive score bounds via `(` prefix supported, SCAN TYPE filter, EXPIRETIME/PEXPIRETIME precision fix, MSETNX/COPY/OBJECT conformance fixes, ZRANDMEMBER with COUNT/WITHSCORES, HRANDFIELD with COUNT/WITHVALUES, ZADD NX/XX/GT/LT/CH options, LPOS with RANK/COUNT/MAXLEN, BITOP operation validation fix, ZRANGE REV rank-mode fix (now uses descending index like Redis), SORT_RO STORE rejection fix, OBJECT ENCODING intset detection for all-integer sets; missing: full blocking semantics, full Pub/Sub message delivery |

exclusive score bounds via `(` prefix supported, SCAN TYPE filter, EXPIRETIME/PEXPIRETIME precision fix, MSETNX/COPY/OBJECT conformance fixes, ZRANDMEMBER with COUNT/WITHSCORES, HRANDFIELD with COUNT/WITHVALUES, ZADD NX/XX/GT/LT/CH options, LPOS with RANK/COUNT/MAXLEN, BITOP operation validation fix, ZRANGE REV rank-mode fix (now uses descending index like Redis), SORT_RO STORE rejection fix, OBJECT ENCODING intset detection for all-integer sets, Lua table.sort/table.insert/table.remove mutation write-back fix; missing: full blocking semantics, full Pub/Sub message delivery |
 | Core data types and keyspace | in_progress | String, Hash, List, Set, Sorted Set, HyperLogLog, and Geo data types implemented with full WRONGTYPE enforcement; Streams fully implemented (`XADD`, `XLEN`, `XDEL`, `XTRIM`, `XREAD`, `XREADGROUP`, `XCLAIM`, `XAUTOCLAIM`, `XPENDING`, `XACK`, `XSETID`, `XINFO STREAM/GROUPS/CONSUMERS`, `XGROUP CREATE/DESTROY/SETID/CREATECONSUMER/DELCONSUMER`, `XRANGE`, `XREVRANGE`) |
 | TTL and eviction behavior | in_progress | lazy expiry and `PTTL` semantics scaffolded (`-2/-1/remaining`) |
 | RDB/AOF persistence | in_progress | AOF record frame contract implemented; Store→AOF rewrite serialization (all data types + TTL), atomic file I/O (write_aof_file/read_aof_file), Runtime::load_aof replay, SAVE/BGSAVE/BGREWRITEAOF wired to actual persistence; RDB snapshot format pending |
 | Replication baseline | in_progress | state/offset progression scaffolded; protocol sync semantics pending |
 | ACL/config mode split | in_progress | ACL command subsystem implemented (AUTH, ACL SETUSER/GETUSER/DELUSER/LIST/WHOAMI/CAT/GENPASS/LOG); CONFIG GET/SET implemented in fr-runtime with full glob pattern matching and multi-pattern support; full parameter surface pending |

@@ -1457,6 +1457,150 @@
         "kind": "error",
         "value": "ERR wrong number of arguments for 'SCRIPT' command"
       }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "eval_table_sort_numbers",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "argv": [
+        "EVAL",
+        "local t = {3, 1, 2}\ntable.sort(t)\nreturn t[1]",
+        "0"
+      ],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 1
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "eval_table_sort_returns_ordered",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "argv": [
+        "EVAL",
+        "local t = {5, 3, 1, 4, 2}\ntable.sort(t)\nreturn t[1] * 10000 + t[2] * 1000 + t[3] * 100 + t[4] * 10 + t[5]",
+        "0"
+      ],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 12345
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "eval_table_insert_append",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "argv": [
+        "EVAL",
+        "local t = {10, 20}\ntable.insert(t, 30)\nreturn t[3]",
+        "0"
+      ],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 30
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "eval_table_insert_at_position",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "argv": [
+        "EVAL",
+        "local t = {1, 3}\ntable.insert(t, 2, 2)\nreturn t[1] * 100 + t[2] * 10 + t[3]",
+        "0"
+      ],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 123
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "eval_table_remove_last",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "argv": [
+        "EVAL",
+        "local t = {10, 20, 30}\nlocal v = table.remove(t)\nreturn v",
+        "0"
+      ],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 30
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "eval_table_remove_shrinks_table",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "argv": [
+        "EVAL",
+        "local t = {10, 20, 30}\ntable.remove(t)\nreturn #t",
+        "0"
+      ],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 2
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "eval_table_remove_at_position",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "argv": [
+        "EVAL",
+        "local t = {1, 2, 3}\ntable.remove(t, 1)\nreturn t[1] * 10 + t[2]",
+        "0"
+      ],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 23
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "eval_table_insert_then_sort",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "argv": [
+        "EVAL",
+        "local t = {3, 1}\ntable.insert(t, 2)\ntable.sort(t)\nreturn t[1] * 100 + t[2] * 10 + t[3]",
+        "0"
+      ],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 123
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "eval_table_sort_strings",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "argv": [
+        "EVAL",
+        "local t = {'c', 'a', 'b'}\ntable.sort(t)\nreturn t[1] .. t[2] .. t[3]",
+        "0"
+      ],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "bulk",
+        "value": "abc"
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "eval_table_insert_multiple",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "argv": [
+        "EVAL",
+        "local t = {}\ntable.insert(t, 'a')\ntable.insert(t, 'b')\ntable.insert(t, 'c')\nreturn #t",
+        "0"
+      ],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 3
+      }
+    },
+    {
+      "name": "eval_table_sort_with_redis_data",
+      "now_ms": 0,
+      "argv": [
+        "EVAL",
+        "redis.call('RPUSH', KEYS[1], '3', '1', '2')\nlocal vals = redis.call('LRANGE', KEYS[1], 0, -1)\nlocal nums = {}\nfor i, v in ipairs(vals) do nums[i] = tonumber(v) end\ntable.sort(nums)\nreturn nums[1]",
+        "1",
+        "sortkey"
+      ],
+      "expect": {
+        "kind": "integer",
+        "value": 1
+      }
     }
   ]
 }
